fortinet.fortios.fortios_configuration_f.short_description: Retrieve Facts of FortiOS Configurable Objects.
fortinet.fortios.fortios_configuration_f.description: Collects facts from network devices running the fortios operating system. This module places the facts gathered in the fact tree keyed by the respective resource name. This facts module will only collect those facts which user specified in playbook.

access_token.description: Token-based authentication. Generated from GUI of Fortigate.

vdom.description: Virtual domain, among those defined previously. A vdom is a virtual instance of the FortiGate that can be configured and used as a different unit.

filters.description: A list of expressions to filter the returned results. The items of the list are combined as LOGICAL AND with operator ampersand. One item itself could be concatenated with a comma as LOGICAL OR.

sorters.description: A list of expressions to sort the returned results. The items of the list are in ascending order with operator ampersand. One item itself could be in decending order with a comma inside.

formatters.description: A list of fields to display for returned results.

selectors.description: a list of selector for retrieving the fortigate facts

selector.description: selector for retrieving the fortigate facts

params.description: the parameter for each selector, see definition in above list.

fortinet.fortios.fortios_export_config_p.short_description: Collect the current configurations of the modules and convert then into playbooks.
fortinet.fortios.fortios_export_config_p.description: Collect the current configurations of a module on a running device and convert the returned facts into a playbook that users can apply directly. More than one playbook will be generated if there are many selectors provided.

output_path.description: the path used for saving the playbook.

access_token.description: Token-based authentication. Generated from GUI of Fortigate.

vdom.description: Virtual domain, among those defined previously. A vdom is a virtual instance of the FortiGate that can be configured and used as a different unit.

filters.description: A list of expressions to filter the returned results. The items of the list are combined as LOGICAL AND with operator ampersand. One item itself could be concatenated with a comma as LOGICAL OR.

sorters.description: A list of expressions to sort the returned results. The items of the list are in ascending order with operator ampersand. One item itself could be in decending order with a comma inside.

formatters.description: A list of fields to display for returned results.

selectors.description: A list of selectors used to fetch the current configurations and export the playbook.

selector.description: Module name that used to fetch the current configurations and export the playbook.

params.description: the parameter for each selector, see definition in above list.

access_token.description: Token-based authentication. Generated from GUI of Fortigate.

vdom.description: Virtual domain, among those defined previously. A vdom is a virtual instance of the FortiGate that can be configured and used as a different unit.

filters.description: A list of expressions to filter the returned results. The items of the list are combined as LOGICAL AND with operator ampersand. One item itself could be concatenated with a comma as LOGICAL OR.

sorters.description: A list of expressions to sort the returned results. The items of the list are in ascending order with operator ampersand. One item itself could be in decending order with a comma inside.

formatters.description: A list of fields to display for returned results.

selectors.description: A list of selectors for retrieving the log type.

selector.description: selector of the retrieved log type

params.description: the parameter for each selector, see definition in above list.

fortinet.fortios.fortios_monitor_fact.short_description: Retrieve Facts of FortiOS Monitor Objects.
fortinet.fortios.fortios_monitor_fact.description: Collects monitor facts from network devices running the fortios operating system. This facts module will only collect those facts which user specified in playbook.

access_token.description: Token-based authentication. Generated from GUI of Fortigate.

vdom.description: Virtual domain, among those defined previously. A vdom is a virtual instance of the FortiGate that can be configured and used as a different unit.

filters.description: A list of expressions to filter the returned results. The items of the list are combined as LOGICAL AND with operator ampersand. One item itself could be concatenated with a comma as LOGICAL OR.

sorters.description: A list of expressions to sort the returned results. The items of the list are in ascending order with operator ampersand. One item itself could be in decending order with a comma inside.

formatters.description: A list of fields to display for returned results.

selectors.description: A list of selectors for retrieving the fortiOS facts.

selector.description: selector of the retrieved fortiOS facts.

params.description: the parameter for each selector, see definition in above list.

Examples include all parameters and values need to be adjusted to datasources before usage. Tested with FOS v6.0.0

member_state.description: Add or delete a member under specified attribute path. When member_state is specified, the state option is ignored.

state.description: Indicates whether to create or remove the object.

Examples include all parameters and values need to be adjusted to datasources before usage. Tested with FOS v6.0.0

access_token.description: Token-based authentication. Generated from GUI of Fortigate.

vdom.description: Virtual domain, among those defined previously. A vdom is a virtual instance of the FortiGate that can be configured and used as a different unit.

member_path.description: Member attribute path to operate on. Delimited by a slash character if there are more than one attribute. Parameter marked with member_path is legitimate for doing member operation.

member_state.description: Add or delete a member under specified attribute path. When member_state is specified, the state option is ignored.
